title: What if every character had their personalities inverted
display_title: What if every character had their personalities inverted
wikitext:
[]: What if every character had their personalities inverted?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sun Mar 14, 2010 10:29 pm Do you still think it would be interesting to watch NGE if the characters were completely opposite of what they were like in the show? Imagine, if you will, a world where Evangelion was a cheerful anime, with: Shinji, the hotblooded arrogant hero with a bit of a perverted streak. Rei, the cheery cloudcuckoolander/ loudmouth pilot with hatred of following orders Asuka, the shy, kind-hearted girl that hates to fight. Misato, the reserved, responsible-to-the-point-of-OCD Major. Gendo, the genius leader of NERV and a overprotective father. Those were most of the important ones, but what if you completely inverted the characters? I don't just mean to the point where, for example, Shinji grows a Marty Stu-style spine, I mean if you turn him into a less lucky version of Kamina, with most of the same flaws. Again, that's just an example of the type of extreme inversion I mean.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 15, 2010 1:32 am ~Ahem~ [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8286[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Mon Mar 15, 2010 1:57 am Ritsuko is an idiot that is terrible with calculations and computers. She is also obsessed with dogs. Kaji is a celibate gentleman. Toji is a wimpy sissy. Kensuke is a sports jock who hates technology. Hikari is a pushover and a useless class clown. The old teacher is the best teacher in the country who has numerous awards and respect from every student. I guess Yui was a horrible neglectful mother who hated Shinji and almost dropped him over a bridge trying to scratch a lottery ticket. When Shinji was a toddler she wanted him to watch her disappear in the sychronization test in order to scar him for life because she thought it would be funny upset him. Yui's purpose is to create a future in which Shinji will be miserable

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Mar 15, 2010 2:08 am Sammaeloo wrote: Toji is a wimpy sissy. Hikari is a pushover and a useless class clown.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 15, 2010 3:59 am @Sammaeloo: Depending on who you ask (not me) , you've just described the original Yui to a tee.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Mon Mar 15, 2010 5:09 am Maya would be bloodthirsty, Aoba would probably be married with kids, Hyuga would a stereotypical alpha male and Kaworu would kill kittens [ Oh wait... ]

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Mon Mar 15, 2010 5:34 am Halicat wrote: Maya would be bloodthirsty [ Oh wait... ]

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Mon Mar 15, 2010 6:12 am Justice served: Yui's a Trap [oh wait...]

[]: Re: What if every character had their personalities inverted             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Mon Mar 15, 2010 9:41 am Xard wrote: CyberXIII wrote: Shinji, the hotblooded arrogant hero with a bit of a perverted streak. Gendo, the genius leader of NERV and a overprotective father.

[]: Re: What if every character had their personalities inverted             Quote Post by Halicat » Mon Mar 15, 2010 9:50 am CyberXIII wrote: I highly doubt they're either of those.

Gendo, the genius leader of NERV and a overprotective father.

[]: Re: What if every character had their personalities inverted             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Mar 15, 2010 11:31 am Xard wrote: HOW ONE EARTH YOU THEN COULDN'T RELATE TO Shinji!?!?!?!?!?

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Mon Mar 15, 2010 11:45 am RE: CyberXIII Gendo as HRG...yeah, I can see that.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Mon Mar 15, 2010 3:27 pm Leliel would be the only angel with an AT Field that keeps things out, all the others would act like the Festum from Fafner.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Mon Mar 15, 2010 5:07 pm I GOT ONE: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Mar 15, 2010 5:09 pm the answer is, it just wouldn't be eva, things would turn out well. eva's hallmark was that of the tragic.
